narration: Create an integer variable named userScore and initialize it to zero.
Create an integer variable named cpuScore and initialize it to zero.
Create an infinite while loop using the condition while(true).
Print a prompt asking the user to enter 1 for rock, 2 for paper, or 3 for scissors.
Import the Scanner class using import java.util.Scanner.
Create a Scanner object named scan and pass System.in as the parameter.
Create an integer variable named userChoice and assign it the value of scan.nextInt().
Import the Random class using import java.util.Random.
Create a Random object named rand with no parameters.
Create an integer variable named cpuChoice and assign it the value of rand.nextInt(3) + 1 to generate a random number between 1 and 3.
Create an if statement to check if cpuChoice equals userChoice.
Inside the tie condition, print a message stating it is a tie and no points are awarded.
Create an else if statement to check if userChoice equals 1.
Inside the userChoice equals 1 block, create an if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 2.
If cpuChoice is 2, print that the CPU chose paper and the user lost, then increment cpuScore.
Use System.out.printf to display the current cpuScore and userScore with newline characters.
Create an else if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 3.
If cpuChoice is 3, print that the CPU chose scissors and the user won, then increment userScore.
Display the updated scores using printf.
Create an else if statement to check if userChoice equals 2.
Inside the userChoice equals 2 block, create an if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 1.
If cpuChoice is 1, print that the CPU chose rock and the user won, then increment userScore.
Display the updated scores using printf.
Create an else if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 3.
If cpuChoice is 3, print that the CPU chose scissors and the user lost, then increment cpuScore.
Display the updated scores using printf.
Create an else if statement to check if userChoice equals 3.
Inside the userChoice equals 3 block, create an if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 1.
If cpuChoice is 1, print that the CPU chose rock and the user lost, then increment cpuScore.
Display the updated scores using printf.
Create an else if statement to check if cpuChoice equals 2.
If cpuChoice is 2, print that the CPU chose paper and the user won, then increment userScore.
Display the updated scores using printf.
Create an if statement to check if cpuScore equals 2.
If cpuScore is 2, print a message stating the user lost best two out of three.
Display the final scores using printf.
Use the break statement to exit the infinite while loop.
Create an if statement to check if userScore equals 2.
If userScore is 2, print a message congratulating the user for winning best two out of three.
Display the final scores using printf.
Use the break statement to exit the infinite while loop.
